summary: Hell‑in‑A‑Bucket opener and fiery Scarlet>Fire dominate a solid show
blurb: The set opens with a cracking Hell In A Bucket that sets a high‑energy tone. Highlights include the seamless Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain segue, a tight Playing In The Band jam, and an enthusiastic Not Fade Away finale. The Mighty Quinn closes the night on an upbeat note.
review: Ventura County Fairgrounds on June 14, 1987 delivered a bright‑sunny Dead show that swings from a punchy opener into a second‑set that many fans still talk about. Jerry kicked things off with a “cracking” Hell In A Bucket, his lead lines sharp and the band instantly locked in. Brent held down solid keys on the first half while Bill and Mickey turned the Rhythm Devils section into a propulsive engine, especially during the extended Playing In The Band jam that segued into a soaring Terrapin Station. The Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain transition was a particular highlight, with the band weaving a seamless, fiery tapestry that kept the crowd dancing. Not Fade Away closed the second set on a high‑octane note, and an exuberant The Mighty Quinn (Quinn The Eskimo) sent fans home smiling. While a few mixes suffer from bright highs or occasional flatness, the performance itself remains strong throughout, making this show a worthwhile listen for anyone chasing that late‑summer West Coast vibe.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Across three recordings the sentiment is mixed but leans positive: two reviews praise standout songs and tight playing, while one notes occasional flatness or mix issues. Positive language such as "cracking" performance, "tight" Hell In A Bucket, "stellar" Scarlet>Fire, and enthusiastic second‑set jams outweigh the limited negative comments about specific mix problems.

best_recording.reason: Full‑show soundboard capture with the most complete set and clear representation of the standout second‑set, despite a bright high‑end mix.

key_highlights: Cracking Hell In A Bucket opener sets an energetic tone.
Seamless Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain segue energizes the second set.
Tight Playing In The Band jam leads into a soaring Terrapin Station.
Not Fade Away finale and upbeat Mighty Quinn encore close the night.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers a cracking lead on Hell In A Bucket and stays central throughout, with strong solos even if his voice sounded "sapped up" at times.
band_performance.Phil: In great form during Hell In A Bucket, providing solid bass foundation for the jams.
band_performance.Bob: Adds energetic yelps especially on Not Fade Away; overall steady rhythm guitar work despite no specific solo highlights noted.
band_performance.Brent: Solid keyboard work anchoring the first set and interacting smoothly with Jerry during the jam sections.
band_performance.Mickey: Part of the Rhythm Devils duo, praised for driving the extended jams in the second set.
band_performance.Billy: Keeps the groove tight; a unique drum moment at 09:13 highlighted as a standout.
